title: Task
description: Owl task.yaml payload. Tracker metadata (`status`, `labels`) added in the P1-A tracker iteration. The schema is deliberately permissive (additionalProperties: true, no required fields) so legacy task.yaml files written before a field existed still validate: a missing `status` reads as `open` and a missing `labels` reads as `[]` at read time. Validation is wired on tracker mutations (create, set-status, label add/rm) to reject malformed values rather than silently persisting them.

workflow.description: Workflow snapshot mapping (with a `key`) for live tasks, or a bare key string in some legacy/index projections.

status.description: Explicit, task-level lifecycle status. Orthogonal to step progress (set by the agent/user, never derived from steps). Default `open` at create. `archived` is set systemically by `owl archive`; `abandoned` by `owl task abandon`. The other values are user-settable via `owl task set-status`.

labels.description: Free-form labels for grouping and filtering. Mutated idempotently via `owl task label add|rm`; deduplicated and trimmed.

blocked_by.description: Cross-task dependency edge: ids of tasks that must reach a terminal status (`done`/`archived`) before this task is considered ready. Canonical direction — reverse `blocks`/dependents are not stored, but computed by reverse-scanning the index. Mutated via `owl task dep add|rm`, which rejects self-dependencies and cycles. A missing `blocked_by` reads as `[]` for legacy task.yaml.
